steps.title: Find the quantity clue
steps.content: The passage contrasts Sheppard's team finding over one hundred satellites with other astronomers who find only one or two. The blank must describe discoveries that are numerous — a large quantity, not a quality or probability.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — inferior: Inferior means lower in quality or rank; the passage compares quantities (100+ vs. 1–2), not the quality of the discoveries. ✗
  • Choice B — random: Random means without pattern or purpose; this says nothing about how many satellites were found. ✗
  • Choice C — plentiful: Plentiful means existing in large amounts, which precisely fits a team that has found over one hundred satellites while others find one or two. ✓
  • Choice D — likely: Likely means probable; it describes expectation rather than the actual quantity of discoveries already made. ✗
That is the correct answer.
